title: Building a mould and deckle for hand papermaking
materials: brass mesh or fiberglass window screen
sources: Dard Hunter, Old Papermaking (1923, public domain) — mould construction traditions; wove and laid mould types
notes: Building a home A5 or A4 mould and deckle. The mould is a mesh-covered frame; the deckle is an identical frame with no mesh that sits on top to define the sheet edge. Covers: frame jointing, mesh choice (wove mesh for smooth surface, laid wire for the traditional chain-line pattern), stretching and tacking the mesh, and building the matching deckle. Tools from woodworking; no specialist equipment.
